summary: Sagittal molar relationships indirectly affect arch form via skeletal changes influencing jaw position, while Bolton discrepancies primarily impact spacing and crowding rather than directly determining arch shape. Neither has a definitive role in establishing baseline arch form; their effects depend heavily on individual patient factors and treatment goals.
